pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 14 generates CSV file at this exact path with content verified
setup_task.sh policy CSV lines 16-20 define these 4 parts with StatusRecommendation='Active'
setup_task.sh policy CSV line 19 defines SP-FUSE-OLD with StatusRecommendation='Obsolete'

Code analysis: setup_task.sh seed_data (lines 82-88) provides current stock; policy CSV provides NewMin/NewMax. Task logic orders when Current<NewMin with qty=NewMax-Current. Verified: SP-BELT-V46 Current=2<NewMin=5→18; SP-SENS-T10 Current=4<NewMin=5→11; SP-VALVE-05 Current=0<NewMin=2→5. SP-FILT-2020 (Current=15≥NewMin=10) and SP-FUSE-OLD (Obsolete) correctly excluded.

privileged_info_summary: The inventory policy file is located at /home/ga/Desktop/inventory_policy.csv and contains 5 spare parts with updated minimum/maximum stock thresholds. Four parts (SP-BELT-V46, SP-FILT-2020, SP-SENS-T10, SP-VALVE-05) are designated as Active and should have thresholds updated. One part (SP-FUSE-OLD) is marked Obsolete and should not have thresholds updated. Based on current stock levels (SP-BELT-V46: 2, SP-FILT-2020: 15, SP-SENS-T10: 4, SP-VALVE-05: 0) compared to new minimums, a work order must include three parts: SP-BELT-V46 with quantity 18, SP-SENS-T10 with quantity 11, and SP-VALVE-05 with quantity 5. SP-FILT-2020 (already at sufficient stock) and SP-FUSE-OLD (obsolete) should not be ordered.
